name: OPENPGP
author: Paulhenry Saux & Community
description: End-to-end OpenPGP for webmail: sign and encrypt outgoing messages, and automatically verify signatures and decrypt incoming PGP mail. Private keys are encrypted at rest with a passphrase, and unlocked into non-extractable WebCrypto keys that never leave your browser. Runs in the privileged (same-origin) plugin tier so all cryptography happens locally.

autoImportSignerCerts.label: Auto-save signer certificates
autoImportSignerCerts.description: When a validly signed message is opened, remember the signer's certificate so you can later send them encrypted mail without importing it manually.

encryptDrafts.label: Encrypt drafts and uploaded attachments
encryptDrafts.description: Encrypt drafts and attachments before saving them to the server. This is useful if you want to keep your drafts private, but it may cause issues if you activated the 'Encrypt on append' option in your stalwart account

askForDefaultKeyPassOnActivated.label: Ask for the passphrase when booting the app.
askForDefaultKeyPassOnActivated.description: Ask for the passphrase decrypting your default key when booting the app. Avoid to go to settings to manually unlock your key.

lockOnLogout.label: Lock keys on logout
lockOnLogout.description: Wipe all unlocked private keys from memory when you sign out or switch accounts. Leave on unless you have a specific reason not to.

defaultEncrypt.label: Default encrypt
defaultEncrypt.description: Whether to encrypt messages by default.

defaultSign.label: Default sign
defaultSign.description: Whether to sign messages by default.

en.banner.locked: Encrypted — unlock your key to read
en.settings.title: OpenPGP keys
en.settings.private_keys_desc: Import an armored OpenPGP private key (.asc/.key). The key remains encrypted locally in your browser sandbox.
en.settings.public_keys_desc: Public keys (ASCII Armored) of contacts you send encrypted mail to.
en.toolbar.action.encrypt_title: Encrypt this message to its recipients
en.banner.encrypted_locked: Encrypted — unlock your PGP key to read
en.prompt.unlock_default_key.title: Unlock your default key
en.prompt.unlock_default_key.message: Unlock your default key to unlock all features of PGP plugin
en.prompt.import_private_key.title: Import Private Key
en.prompt.import_private_key.message: Provide the current passphrase to decrypt your OpenPGP file, and optionally set a new one for local storage optimization.
en.prompt.unlock_key.title: Unlock your key
en.prompt.unlock_key.message_suffix: ) to unlock all features of the PGP plugin
en.error.no_default_public_key_attachment: No default public key found for attachment encryption. Please set a default public key in OpenPGP settings.
en.error.not_privileged_tier: Cannot sign/encrypt: OpenPGP is not running in the privileged tier.
en.error.no_key_for_email_suffix: . Import one in Settings → Plugins → OpenPGP.
en.error.key_locked: Your OpenPGP key is locked. Unlock it in Settings, then resend.
en.error.cryptographic_failed: Cryptographic processing failed to generate an output envelope.
en.error.unknown_public_key_prefix: Unknown public key or missing from the keyring (Key ID:
en.error.decrypt_failed_prefix: Could not decrypt this PGP message:
en.error.metadata_parse_failed: Erreur lors du parsing des métadonnées PGP :
en.warn.metadata_missing_attachments: No metadata found for attachments, but attachments exist. They may not be restored correctly.
